meta.description: Crypto and Security packages for Standard Library 2.0

objective: Implement panther.crypto and panther.security packages for cryptographic operations and security utilities

panther_implemented_logic.description: All 47 functions implemented in .pan files under stdlib/panther/crypto/__init__.pan and stdlib/panther/security/__init__.pan
panther_implemented_logic.pattern: Self-hosted PantherLang wrappers that delegate to Python-backed stdlib primitives

host_abi_primitives.description: No new Host ABI primitives required. All package functions delegate to existing Python-backed stdlib functions which use existing Host ABI backends where available.

native_backends.description: No new native backends. Existing backends (libc, libcrypto) used transitively via existing stdlib functions.

python_fallbacks.description: All functions delegate to Python-backed stdlib functions in compiler/stdlib/functions.py which have native fallbacks where applicable.

full_regression.note: All existing test suites pass without regression

example_results.description: Package functions verified working in PantherLang programs

known_limitations: No native PBKDF2 — implemented as iterated SHA256 (not standard PBKDF2)
No AES/ChaCha20 — symmetric encryption not implemented
No X.509/cert handling — certificate management not implemented
Security package uses string concatenation for structured data (no objects)
Rate limiting is stub (no persistent storage)
No JWT handling — token validation not implemented
No TLS/SSL cert handling — certificate validation not implemented
Input validation regex may not cover all edge cases
next_phase: Phase 8 — Testing and Concurrency (panther.testing, panther.concurrent, panther.async)
